mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, with interaction term coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero at p-value greater than 0.05 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without price pressures, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of low-inflation periods, or the mechanism reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income, supporting corporate profit margins and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all historical regimes, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 325.0, it indicates entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 60-day returns in more than 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 325.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real GDP growth outpaces industrial production signals robust service-sector demand that compensates for manufacturing softness, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDP-INDPRO spread shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 and no significant directional bias in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During liquidity crises, sudden federal funds rate cuts provide immediate relief to leveraged balance sheets, triggering rapid risk-on repricing and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rate cuts during stress periods fail to generate positive 5-day returns, with mean forward return remaining negative or statistically insignificant at p-value greater than 0.10.
